research_question: Under the frozen trainval-only contract and execution semantics, does reversal_p98_cord30_ew_v1 outperform reversal_tail_exclude_p98_v1 at the fixed-test / validation layer without introducing new robustness failures?
change_detail: Single frozen challenger only: add cord30 to the p98 tail-handled reversal baseline through equal-weight percentile-rank averaging. No contract change, no refresh-rule change, no new data, no weight optimization, no second candidate.

reversal_p98_cord30_ew_v1.name: p98 reversal + cord30 equal-weight rank composite

frozen_construction.step_1: Negate reversal raw score and apply daily p98 exclusion exactly as in the previous round
frozen_construction.step_2: Join with cord30 score under the same sample universe
frozen_construction.step_3: Per signal_date compute PERCENT_RANK for p98 reversal and cord30 separately
frozen_construction.step_4: Average the two rank scores equally
frozen_construction.step_5: Use the composite score for standard TopK=10 run-state ranking

disallowed_changes: Do not add a second composite candidate.
Do not change the p98 exclusion rule.
Do not change TopK, contract, execution semantics, or cost stress assumptions.
Do not reopen data acquisition during this round.
serial_rule: Only one challenger is allowed. Run the formal baseline and the challenger under the same frozen contract, then make a single promote / reject decision.
